sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Mrs. Wood, a 43-year-old female, presented with hematuria, which she attributes to kidney stones.

She reports passing two small stones last week, and for the past 6 weeks, she has experienced episodes of bright red urine, described as the color of cherry cholestee, and has had a constant urge to urinate with severe pain, rated an 8 out of 10.
The patient denies any flank pain or tenderness in her lower back after passing the stones.
She takes Alavert 10 mg daily and has no known drug allergies.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: Alavert 10 mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's abdomen is normal, with no flank pain, and the vaginal exam is normal.
Test Results: The urine dipstick was negative for leukocytes, nitrates, protein, ketones, bilirubin, and glucose.
The urine was yellow and clear.
Urobilinogen was 0.2, pH was 5.5, and specific gravity was 1.020.
The renal ultrasound showed a right kidney length of 10 cm by 5 cm wide with no stones, masses, or hydronephrosis.
The left kidney was 10.8 cm in length and 5 cm wide, with a 6 mm nonobstructing left renal stone.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has hematuria due to a 6 mm nonobstructing left renal stone, which she passed two small stones last week.
Problem list: Hematuria; Chronic urolithiasis; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I advised the patient to drink 125 ounces of water per day, avoid sugary drinks like Mountain Dew, and reduce her sodium intake to less than 1000 mg per day.
I also recommended avoiding food rich in sodium, oxalate, and animal protein.
I provided her with a handout outlining the dietary recommendations and what to avoid.
I instructed her to return in a year unless she experiences any further issues with bleeding or pain, in which case she can call the office sooner.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen in a year unless she experiences any further issues, at which point she can call the office sooner.
